# iru_custom_script (Resource)

Manages an Iru Custom Script library item.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "iru_custom_script" "hello" {
  name                = "Hello World"
  execution_frequency = "once"
  script              = file("${path.module}/hello.sh")
  active              = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `execution_frequency` (String) How often the script runs. One of `once`, `every_15_min`, `every_day`, `no_enforcement`.
- `name` (String) Name of the custom script.
- `script` (String) The script body.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the script is active. Defaults to the server value.
- `remediation_script` (String) Optional remediation script body. The API returns an empty string when unset; leaving this unconfigured adopts that value.
- `restart` (Boolean) Whether to restart after running.
- `self_service_category_id` (String) Self Service category ID (from the `iru_self_service_categories` data source). Write-only in the API: it is accepted on write but not returned, so the configured value is preserved in state.
- `self_service_recommended` (Boolean) Whether the script is marked recommended in Self Service. Write-only in the API (see `self_service_category_id`).
- `show_in_self_service` (Boolean) Whether the script is offered in Self Service.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Unique identifier of the custom script.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Custom scripts are imported by their library item ID.
terraform import iru_custom_script.hello 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
